spec.description: Total rate-limit decisions per second across all selected instances.
spec.title: Total RPS

spec.description: Share of decisions that returned denied.
spec.title: Denial Rate

spec.description: p99 latency of hot-path origin fetches (Redis GET for cold, stale, or strict-mode windows). This adds directly to request latency.
spec.title: Origin Fetch p99 (all fetch ops)

spec.description: CAS retry exhaustion over the selected range. Expected value: 0. Any non-zero reading is an anomaly worth investigating.
spec.title: CAS Exhausted (range)

spec.description: Decisions per second broken out by source (local vs origin) and outcome (passed vs denied).
spec.title: Decisions by Source & Outcome

spec.description: Passed and denied share of total decisions. A sudden drop in passed rate usually indicates a caller hitting a new, lower limit or abuse.
spec.title: Pass / Deny Ratio

spec.description: Latency of hot-path Redis GETs split into fetch_cold, fetch_stale, and fetch_strict operations. This latency lands on the request critical path.
spec.title: Origin Fetch Latency (hot path)

spec.description: Latency of replay-worker Redis INCRs. Slow syncs mean nodes take longer to converge on shared counters.
spec.title: Origin Sync Latency (replay)

spec.description: Fraction of decisions resolved from local state without a Redis fetch. Lower values mean more hot-path Redis pressure.
spec.title: Local Decision Share

spec.description: Origin operations per second by op from unkey_ratelimit_origin_operations_total. Fetch volume is direct hot-path Redis GET load; sync volume tracks replay throughput.
spec.title: Origin Operations / sec

spec.description: Origin errors per second broken out by op and reason. Sustained fetch timeouts loosen cross-node convergence because decisions fall back to local state.
spec.title: Origin Errors

spec.description: Error rate as a share of origin operations, by op. Denominator uses unkey_ratelimit_origin_operations_total so operation counts and error rates share the same source.
spec.title: Origin Error Rate

spec.description: Strict-mode activations per second. Spikes correlate with sustained denial pressure and will increase origin fetch latency on the hot path.
spec.title: Strict Mode Activations

spec.description: Ratelimit() calls that exhausted CAS retries and failed closed. Expected to be flat at zero. Any activity indicates pathological contention.
spec.title: CAS Exhausted Rate

spec.description: Sliding-window counters held in memory per instance. Drives cardinality and memory footprint.
spec.title: Active Windows

spec.description: Creation vs eviction rate of sliding windows. Sustained divergence (created > evicted) is a leading indicator of memory growth.
spec.title: Window Churn (created vs evicted)

spec.description: Cross-region rows pulled in the most recent sync tick (one row per active window cell, summed across regions in SQL).
spec.title: Active Cross-Region Rows

spec.description: Rate of writes to ratelimit_global_counters. Driven by entries crossing the 50% utilization floor; not request volume.
spec.title: Cross-Region Writes

spec.description: Failed cross-region flushes over the selected range. Expected: 0. Sustained non-zero values mean this fleet's counts are not reaching MySQL and other regions are deciding on stale state.
spec.title: Write Errors (range)

spec.description: Failed cross-region sync ticks over the selected range. Expected: 0. Non-zero means this region is not pulling fresh counts from peers and is enforcing on stale state.
spec.title: Sync Errors (range)

spec.description: End-to-end cross-region count flow. Writes are this fleet's flushed rows. Sync rows applied is the read-side load: per-key sums other regions returned. Entries created is the subset that opened a brand-new local counter (this node had not seen the key from its own traffic). A big gap between writes and entries created means cross-region knowledge mostly overlaps with locally-active keys.
spec.title: Cross-Region Pipeline

spec.description: Error rates on both sides of the cross-region channel. Write errors mean this fleet's counts are not reaching MySQL. Sync errors mean this fleet is not pulling peer counts. Either side failing degrades cross-region enforcement.
spec.title: Cross-Region Errors
